title: Centaurea daralagoezica
taxonomy: Centaurea daralagoezica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Centaurea, which consists of thistles and knapweeds. This plant is native to Turkey and is known for its distinctive characteristics. Centaurea daralagoezica is typically found in mountainous regions and is often associated with limestone soils. The classification of Centaurea daralagoezica reflects its unique morphology and evolutionary history within the Asteraceae family.
